title: South Korea and US begin combined naval exercise in Yellow Sea
summary: The Korea View reports the ROK Navy and US 7th Fleet launched a combined anti-submarine warfare exercise in the Yellow Sea, deploying destroyers and maritime patrol aircraft.
contents:
# ROK-US Combined Naval Exercise Begins in Yellow Sea

## Exercise Overview
The Republic of Korea Navy (ROKN) and the US 7th Fleet commenced a combined anti-submarine warfare exercise in the Yellow Sea on May 1, 2026. The exercise focuses on detecting and tracking submarine threats in littoral waters.

## Participating Forces
- ROKN Sejong the Great-class destroyer
- ROKN Gwanggaeto the Great-class destroyer
- US Navy Arleigh Burke-class destroyer
- P-3C and P-8A maritime patrol aircraft
- ROK Navy submarines participating as opposing forces

## Exercise Objectives
- Anti-submarine warfare coordination between allied navies
- Maritime domain awareness in contested waters
- Combined command and control procedures
- Interoperability enhancement for contingency operations

## Regional Context
The exercise follows DPRK coastal defense inspections and maintains allied readiness posture on the Korean Peninsula. South Korean defense officials describe the drill as routine and pre-planned.
